content.question: 1. Mr Jones ___ to himself as he read a funny story in the newspaper.
content.final_notes_html:
📚 BẢNG TỔNG HỢP (VERBS OF LAUGHING & SMILING):

1. laugh (v) = to make the sounds and movements which usu express amusement, joy, etc.
2. smile (v) = to turn the corners of the mouth up usu showing happiness, amusement or pleasure.
3. giggle (v) = to laugh lightly in a silly or nervous way.
4. chuckle (v) = to laugh quietly or to oneself.
5. sneer (v) = to make the smile-like expression of the face which expresses contempt or scorn.
6. grin (v) = to smile very broadly, often in a silly or embarrassed way.
7. snigger (v) = to laugh in an unpleasant way, usu at sb's misfortune.
8. smirk (v) = to smile in a self-satisfied way.
9. beam (v) = to smile happily, showing great pleasure.
10. titter (v) = to laugh in a brief, nervous way.
11. guffaw (v) = to laugh loudly (usu used in a derogatory sense).

content.question: 2. The students ___ when they saw that their teacher had sat on some chewing gum.
3. I hardly ever ___ at jokes. I just don't find them funny.
4. The woman ___ with pleasure when she saw her daughter get off the plane.
5. Fiona ___ pleasantly at her neighbour when she saw her walking through the park.
6. When he ___ at the joke, everyone in the theatre turned to look at him.
7. The boy was so embarrassed he could do nothing but ___ speechlessly.
8. It's disgusting the way she ___ at everyone who doesn't dress as well as she does.
9. Mr Smith always ___ when he sees me; I get the feeling he knows something I don't.
10. Those teenage girls do nothing but ___ when they see boys they like.
11. The women ___ at their friend's rather lewd joke.
